Package org.apache.juneau.http.header

Class ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.http.header.ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder
Enclosing class:
ContentSecurityPolicy
public static class ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder extends Object
Fluent builder for assembling a Content-Security-Policy header value.

Composes the directives defined by CSP Level 3 into the directive source source; ... wire format expected by the Content-Security-Policy response header. Typed setters cover the common directives; directive(String, String...) is a generic escape hatch so new directives never require an API change. Setting the same directive more than once replaces the previous source list.

Example:

String value = ContentSecurityPolicy.create() .defaultSrc(SELF) .imgSrc(SELF, "data:") .objectSrc(NONE) .build(); // => "default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; object-src 'none'"

Nonces:

Source helper nonce(String) renders a 'nonce-<token>' source expression. For per-request nonces (where the actual token is not known until the response is rendered), use NONCE_PLACEHOLDER as the token and have the REST layer rewrite it per-response via resolveNonce(String, String); the same nonce is then stamped onto the inline <script>/<style> tags the serializer emits.

See Also:
  • ContentSecurityPolicy
  • Content Security Policy Level 3 (W3C WD)
Since:
10.0.0

    • NONCE_PLACEHOLDER

      public static final String NONCE_PLACEHOLDER
      The placeholder token used in nonce sources when the actual per-request nonce is not yet known.

      strictStarter() emits 'nonce-{nonce}'; the REST layer substitutes the real nonce per-response via resolveNonce(String, String).

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • Builder

      public Builder()

  • Method Details

    • nonce

      public static String nonce(String token)
      Renders a 'nonce-<token>' source expression.
      Parameters:
      token - The base64 nonce value (or NONCE_PLACEHOLDER for deferred substitution). Must not be null or blank.
      Returns:
      The source expression. Never null.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If token is null or blank.

    • scheme

      public static String scheme(String scheme)
      Renders a scheme source expression, ensuring a single trailing colon (e.g. "https" => https:).
      Parameters:
      scheme - The scheme (with or without a trailing colon). Must not be null or blank.
      Returns:
      The scheme source expression. Never null.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If scheme is null or blank.

    • hash

      public static String hash(String algorithm, String base64Hash)
      Renders a 'sha256-<hash>' (or sha384/sha512) source expression.
      Parameters:
      algorithm - One of "sha256", "sha384", or "sha512". Must not be null or blank.
      base64Hash - The base64-encoded hash of the inline block. Must not be null or blank.
      Returns:
      The hash source expression. Never null.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If either argument is null or blank.

    • generateNonce

      public static String generateNonce()
      Mints a fresh, cryptographically-random CSP nonce.

      Generates 128 bits (16 bytes) from SecureRandom encoded as an unpadded base64url string. Each call returns a new value; a nonce must never be reused across responses.

      Returns:
      A new nonce token. Never null.

    • resolveNonce

      public static String resolveNonce(String policyValue, String nonce)
      Replaces every occurrence of NONCE_PLACEHOLDER in a rendered policy value with the supplied nonce.
      Parameters:
      policyValue - The rendered policy value (may contain NONCE_PLACEHOLDER). May be null.
      nonce - The per-request nonce token. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The policy value with placeholders substituted, or null if policyValue was null.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If nonce is null.

    • create

      public static ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder create()
      Creates a new empty builder.
      Returns:
      A new builder. Never null.

    • strictStarter

      public static ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder strictStarter()
      Creates a builder pre-populated with the nonce-based strict starter preset.

      The preset is:

      default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'nonce-{nonce}'; style-src 'self' 'nonce-{nonce}'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'

      The {nonce} tokens are NONCE_PLACEHOLDERs; for the policy to function on Juneau-served HTML (which emits inline <script>/<style>) the REST layer must mint a per-request nonce, substitute it via resolveNonce(String, String), and stamp the same value onto the emitted inline tags.

      Returns:
      A new pre-populated builder. Never null.

    • defaultSrc

      public ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder defaultSrc(String... sources)
      Sets the default-src directive.
      Parameters:
      sources - The source expressions. May be empty.
      Returns:
      This object.

      Sets the script-src directive.

      sources - The source expressions. May be empty.
      Returns:
      This object.

      sources - The source expressions. May be empty.
      Returns:
      This object.

      Sets the img-src directive.

      sources - The source expressions. May be empty.

      sources - The source expressions. May be empty.

      Sets the font-src directive.
      Parameters:
      sources - The source expressions. May be empty.
      Returns:
      This object.

      public ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder objectSrc(String... sources)
      Sets the object-src directive.
      Parameters:
      sources - The source expressions. May be empty.
      Returns:
      This object.

      public ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder baseUri(String... sources)
      Sets the base-uri directive.
      Parameters:
      sources - The source expressions. May be empty.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • frameAncestors

      public ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder frameAncestors(String... sources)
      Sets the frame-ancestors directive.
      Parameters:
      sources - The source expressions. May be empty.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • reportUri

      public ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder reportUri(String... uris)
      Sets the report-uri directive (deprecated in CSP3 but still widely supported).
      Parameters:
      uris - The collector URIs. May be empty.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • reportTo

      public ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder reportTo(String... groups)
      Sets the report-to directive (the CSP3 reporting-group mechanism).
      Parameters:
      groups - The reporting-group names. May be empty.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • directive

      public ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder directive(String name, String... sources)
      Generic escape hatch for setting any directive by name.

      Replaces any previously-registered source list for the same directive while preserving its original position.

      Parameters:
      name - The directive name (e.g. "worker-src"). Must not be null or blank.
      sources - The source expressions. May be empty (renders the bare directive name).
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If name is null or blank.

    • reportOnly

      public ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder reportOnly()
      Flips the rendered header to the observe-only ContentSecurityPolicy.REPORT_ONLY_NAME variant.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • reportOnly

      public ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder reportOnly(boolean value)
      Sets whether the rendered header uses the observe-only ContentSecurityPolicy.REPORT_ONLY_NAME variant.
      Parameters:
      value - true to emit Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only instead of Content-Security-Policy.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • isReportOnly

      public boolean isReportOnly()
      Returns true if this builder is configured to emit the report-only header variant.
      Returns:
      A boolean flag.

    • build

      public String build()
      Builds the rendered policy value (the header value only, not including the header name).

      Returns an empty string when no directives have been registered.

      Returns:
      The policy value. Never null.

    • toHeader

      public ContentSecurityPolicy toHeader()
      Builds a ContentSecurityPolicy header bean from this builder.

      The header name is ContentSecurityPolicy.REPORT_ONLY_NAME when isReportOnly() is true, otherwise ContentSecurityPolicy.NAME.

      Returns:
      A new header bean carrying build(). Never null.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
